element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Strategic use in later tournament rounds
Psychological gameplay element used strategically throughout
Multiple opponent sequences with escalating difficulty
Character-specific move sets and story variations

typical_tension_triggers: Stronger opponents in later tournament rounds
Boss character encounters (Mr. Big, Mr. Karate, Geese Howard)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension-release through best-of-three match format; escalating difficulty through tournament progression; story beats punctuate fights

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic combat mechanics; player-friendly first opponents
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tournament opponents increase in complexity and AI challenge; introduction of character-specific move patterns
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss characters (Mr. Big in AoF1, Geese Howard in AoF2) require mastery of Desperation Moves and taunt mechanics
notable_spikes: Mr. Karate encounter (Art of Fighting 1 finale) - significant difficulty spike with story revelation
Geese Howard battle (Art of Fighting 2) - mafia boss with advanced fighting patterns
Tom Wyler encounter (Art of Fighting 3) - narrative antagonist with specialized mechanics

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player campaign structured as tournament bracket with escalating opponents; story progression tied to match victories; narrative reveals (e.g., Mr. Karate's identity) occur at finale
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Art of Fighting 1: 10-character tournament culminating in Mr. Karate boss fight

notable_level_patterns.description: Each opponent encounter structured as best-of-three format; tension cycles through individual rounds; players must maintain health across multiple rounds against same opponent
Playable characters (Ryo, Robert, new cast in AoF3) have distinct tournament routes with unique opponents and narrative variations; encourages replay for different story perspectives
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Art of Fighting 2 and 3 multi-character campaigns

notable_level_patterns.description: Tournament progression culminates in hidden or narrative boss character (Mr. Big, Mr. Karate, Geese Howard, Tom Wyler); requires beating standard bracket first; provides story payoff
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All three games; cheat codes or second player input unlock boss playability in AoF1
